title: Ukraine Reports Frontline 'Strongest in a Year'; Zelensky Calls for New Peace Talks
detail: Ukrainian officials stated the country's frontline position is the strongest it has been in approximately one year, citing battlefield gains across multiple sectors. President Zelensky simultaneously called for new peace negotiations to begin, indicating a strategy of negotiating from a position of strength while sustaining military pressure.

title: Turkey Moves to Revive Russia-Ukraine Peace Talks; Erdoğan Briefs NATO Chief
detail: Turkey is actively working to revive stalled Russia-Ukraine peace negotiations. President Erdoğan informed NATO Secretary General of Ankara's diplomatic initiative, signaling renewed Turkish mediation efforts. Turkey previously hosted the 2022 Istanbul talks and has maintained dialogue with both sides throughout the conflict.
